Worked from /Users/conmeara/Projects/ui-backlot (the /Users/botbot path given in the trigger does not exist on this machine; this is the actual ui-backlot checkout matching the CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md context supplied).

reference/sources.json → family "browser": tier native-local, availableOnThisMachine:true, single target {label:"chrome-window", app:"Google Chrome", method:"screencapture", notes:"Tab strip, omnibox, window chrome. Page content is ours by design."}, fallbacks:[]. No prior dated static set exists (reference/browser/ only has a motion/2026-07-09/chrome-omnibox-search-load/ dir, no static screenshot set to diff against) — this would have been the first static capture, using the sources.json target label "chrome-window".

What I tried:
1. Confirmed Google Chrome.app is installed (/Applications/Google Chrome.app) and requested computer-use access at "read" tier (browsers are read-only: visible, no click/type — fine for a passive window-chrome shot, no login/interaction needed).
2. Brought Chrome to front (open_application) and took a computer-use screenshot: visually confirmed real, current Chrome window chrome (tab strip, omnibox with "Chrome | chrome://whats-new", profile avatar, extensions icon, traffic lights) on Chrome's own "What's New" onboarding page — no login, no bot check, nothing personal.
3. Could not file it: `screenshot --save_to_disk` returned no filesystem path in the tool result (only an inline image), and repeated filesystem searches (find over /private/tmp, the session scratchpad, ~/Desktop, ~/Downloads, /var/folders) turned up no new file. This subagent's Bash/computer-use appear to run in separate sandboxes without a shared save path in this session.
4. Tried a direct `screencapture -x` via Bash as a workaround — it captured a DIFFERENT screen than the one computer-use controls (Mail.app frontmost, plus an unrelated video file from what looks like a concurrent sibling subagent's session), confirming Bash and computer-use are disjoint environments here. Discarded/deleted that file immediately rather than filing leaked, unrelated, non-consented content.
5. Checked the Mac App Store fallback (rung 3): real Google Chrome is NOT distributed on the Mac App Store (itunes lookup for bundleId com.google.Chrome returns 0 results; search only surfaces unrelated third-party apps with "chrome" in the name). sources.json also lists no fallbacks for this family. Did not proceed to heavier rungs (yt-dlp video frame-mining, press-kit hunting) given the "cheapest meaningful, drift-check not re-ground" scope and that they wouldn't have this filing problem solved anyway since the blocker is local infra, not source availability.

Net: the native-local tier is reachable and Chrome's current window chrome was visually verified as unchanged in general appearance, but no bytes could be persisted to reference/browser/<date>/chrome-window/ in this session, so no dated set was written and no scored diff is possible. Recommend either (a) re-running this capture in a session where computer-use save_to_disk resolves to a Bash-visible path, or (b) manual-inbox: ask the user for a single Chrome window screenshot (cmd+shift+4+space) to file via `node tools/import-reference.mjs --family browser --label chrome-window --image <path> --method manual-inbox`.
